summary: Collaboration-bus appears implemented in bin/stampede.sh as filesystem protocol.json + JSONL ledgers under .stampede/{run_id}/collab/.

Directory/file creation is mostly idempotent, but atomic JSON writes use fixed {path}.tmp (not process-safe) and metaswarm commander telemetry init truncates child-agents.jsonl (not restart-idempotent).
risks: Concurrent init collisions on fixed .tmp names can clobber protocol.json/state.json.
Restarting stampede.sh against same run may wipe telemetry evidence due to 'w' on child-agents.jsonl.
recommendations: Use unique temp names (PID+rand) or lock for atomic JSON writes.
Do not truncate child-agents.jsonl/swarm-state on restart; append restart marker and merge state.
Skip rewriting protocol.json when already initialized for same run_id.
